briefdescription.para: Event Processing finished signal type.
Key event signal type.
Touch signal type.
Wheel signal type.
Visibility changed signal type.
Effect signal type and state.
Keyboard repeat settings changed signal type.

memberdef.type: Signal< void(const std::string &, const std::string &, const Property::Array &)>
memberdef.definition: typedef Signal<void(const std::string&, const std::string&, const Property::Array&)> Dali::DevelWindow::AuxiliaryMessageSignalType
memberdef.name: AuxiliaryMessageSignalType

briefdescription.para: Auxiliary message signal type.
Accessibility Highlight signal type.
Intercept Key event signal type.
Window Moved signal type.
Window orientation changed signal type.
MouseInOutEvent signal type.
MouseRelativeEvent signal type.
Window Moved by Server signal type.
Window Resized by Server signal type.
InsetsChanged signal type.
PointerConstraintsEvent signal type.
Sets position and size of the window. This API guarantees that both moving and resizing of window will appear on the screen at once.

parameterdescription.para: The new window position and size

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the window that the given actor is added to.

simplesect.para: The window the actor is added to or an empty handle if the actor is not added to any window.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted just after the event processing is finished.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when wheel event is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constWheelEvent&event);
window
The window instance
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the window is shown or hidden.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,boolvisible);
window
The window instance
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted for transition effect.

detaileddescription.para:
The transition animation is appeared when the window is shown/hidden. When the animation is started, START signal is emitted. Then the animation is ended, END signal is emitted, too. A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,EffectStatestate,EffectTypetype);
window
The window instance
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted just after the keyboard repeat setting is changed globally.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when window's auxiliary was changed then display server sent the message.

detaileddescription.para: Auxiliary message is sent by display server. When client application added the window's auxiliary hint and if the auxiliary is changed, display server send the auxiliary message. Auxiliary message has the key, value and options.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the window needs to grab or clear accessibility highlight. The highlight indicates that it is an object to interact with the user regardless of focus. After setting the highlight on the object, you can do things that the object can do, such as giving or losing focus.

detaileddescription.para: This signal is emitted by Dali::Accessibility::Component::GrabHighlight and Dali::Accessibility::Component::ClearHighlight
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,boolhighlight);

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the window is moved.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::Window::WindowPositionposition); The parameters are the moved x and y coordinates. and window means this signal was called from what window

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the window orientation is changed.

detaileddescription.para: To emit Window Orientation signal, AddAvailableOrientation() or SetPreferredOrientation() should be called before device is rotated. Most of cases, AddAvailableOrientation() or SetPreferredOrientation() is callled in onCreate().

A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::WindowOrientationorientation); The parameter is the changed window orientation. and window means this signal was called from what window

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when window has been moved by the display server. To make the window move by display server, RequestMoveToServer() should be called. After the moving job is completed, this function will be called.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::Window::WindowPositionposition); The parameters are the moved x and y coordinates. and window means this signal was called from what window

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when window has been resized by the display server. To make the window move by display server, RequestResizeToServer() should be called. After the resizing job is completed, this function will be called.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::Window::WindowPositionposition); The parameters are the resized width and height coordinates. and window means this signal was called from what window

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: Sets parent window of the window.

detaileddescription.para: After setting that, these windows do together when raise-up, lower and iconified/deiconified. Initially, the window is located on top of the parent. The window can go below parent by calling Lower(). If parent's window stack is changed by calling Raise() or Lower(), child windows are located on top of the parent again.

briefdescription.para: Sets parent window of the window.

detaileddescription.para: After setting that, these windows do together when raise-up, lower and iconified/deiconified. This function has the additional flag whether the child is located above or below of the parent.

parameterdescription.para: The flag is whether the child is located above or below of the parent.

briefdescription.para: Unsets parent window of the window.

detaileddescription.para: After unsetting, the window is disconnected his parent window.

briefdescription.para: Gets parent window of the window.

simplesect.para: The parent window of the window

briefdescription.para: Gets current orientation of the window.

simplesect.para: The current window orientation if previously set, or none

briefdescription.para: Gets current physical orientation of the window.

detaileddescription.para: It means current physical rotation angle of the window. If the height of the display device's area is greater than the width, default current orientation is PORTRAIT and current physical orientation angle is 0. If the width of the display device's area is greater than the height, default current orientation is LANDSCAPE and current physical orientation angle is 0.

simplesect.para: The current physical orientation degree of the window. It is one of them as 0, 90, 180 and 270.

briefdescription.para: Sets available orientations of the window.

detaileddescription.para: This API is for setting several orientations one time.

parameterdescription.para: The available orientation list to add

briefdescription.para: Gets the native window id.
Adds a callback that is called when the frame rendering is done by the graphics driver.

parameterdescription.para: The Id to specify the frame. It will be passed when the callback is called.

simplesect.para: A callback of the following type may be used: voidMyFunction(intframeId); This callback will be deleted once it is called.
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.

briefdescription.para: Adds a callback that is called when the frame is displayed on the display.

parameterdescription.para: The Id to specify the frame. It will be passed when the callback is called.

simplesect.para: A callback of the following type may be used: voidMyFunction(intframeId); This callback will be deleted once it is called.
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.

briefdescription.para: Sets window position and size for specific orientation. This api reserves the position and size per orientation to display server. When the device is rotated, the window is moved/resized with the reserved position/size by display server.

parameterdescription.para: The reserved position and size for the orientation

simplesect.para: Currently, it only works when the window's type is WindowType::IME.
To set WindowType::IME, use Application New(... WindowType type), not Window::SetType().
This function is only useful in Tizen world.

briefdescription.para: Requests to display server for the window is moved by display server.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called in mouse down event callback function. After this function is called in mouse down event callback function, the window is moved with mouse move event. When mouse up event happens, the window moved work is finished.

briefdescription.para: Requests to display server for the window is resized by display server.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called in mouse down event callback function. After this function is called in mouse down event callback function, the window is resized with mouse move event. The direction is selected one of eight ways. When mouse up event happens, the window resized work is finished.

parameterdescription.para: it is indicated the window's side or edge for starting point.

briefdescription.para: Enables the floating mode of window.

detaileddescription.para: The floating mode is to support making partial size window easliy. It is useful to make popup style window and this window is always upper than the other normal window. In addition, it is easy to change between popup style and normal style window.
A special display server(as a Tizen display server) supports this mode.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether the window is floating mode or not.

simplesect.para: True if the window is enabled floating mode, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Includes input region.

detaileddescription.para: This function inlcudes input regions. It can be used multiple times and supports multiple regions. It means input region will be extended.
This input is related to mouse and touch event. If device has touch screen, this function is useful. Otherwise device does not have that, we can use it after connecting mouse to the device.

parameterdescription.para: The added region to accept input events.

briefdescription.para: Excludes input region.

detaileddescription.para: This function excludes input regions. It can be used multiple times and supports multiple regions. It means input region will be reduced. Nofice, should be set input area by IncludeInputRegion() before this function is used.
This input is related to mouse and touch event. If device has touch screen, this function is useful. Otherwise device does not have that, we can use it after connecting mouse to the device.

parameterdescription.para: The subtracted region to except input events.

briefdescription.para: Sets the necessary for window rotation Acknowledgement. After this function called, SendRotationCompletedAcknowledgement() should be called to complete window rotation.

detaileddescription.para: This function is supprot that application has the window rotation acknowledgement's control. It means display server waits when application's rotation work is finished. It is useful application has the other rendering engine which works asynchronous. For instance, GlView. It only works on Tizen device.

parameterdescription.para: the flag is true if window rotation acknowledge is sent.

briefdescription.para: send the Acknowledgement to complete window rotation. For this function, SetNeedsRotationCompletedAcknowledgement should be already called with true.
Feed (Send) touch event to window.
Feed (Send) wheel event to window.
Feed (Send) key event to window.

parameterdescription.para: The key event holding the key information.

briefdescription.para: Feed (Send) hover event to window.

parameterdescription.para: The touch point that create a hover event

briefdescription.para: Maximizes window's size.

detaileddescription.para: If this function is called with true, window will be resized with screen size. Otherwise window will be resized with previous size. It is for the window's MAX button in window's border.
It is for client application. If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

parameterdescription.para: If window is maximized or unmaximized.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether the window is maximized or not.

simplesect.para: True if the window is maximized, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets window's maximum size.

detaileddescription.para: It is to set the maximized size when window is maximized or the window's size is increased by RequestResizeToServer(). Although the size is set by this function, window's size can be increased over the limitation by SetPositionSize() or SetSize().
After setting, if Maximize() is called, window is resized with the setting size and move the center.

briefdescription.para: Minimizes window's size.

detaileddescription.para: If this function is called with true, window will be iconified. Otherwise window will be activated. It is for the window's MIN button in window border.
It is for client application. If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

parameterdescription.para: If window is minimized or unminimized(activated).

briefdescription.para: Returns whether the window is minimized or not.

simplesect.para: True if the window is minimized, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets window's minimum size.

detaileddescription.para: It is to set the minimum size when window's size is decreased by RequestResizeToServer(). Although the size is set by this function, window's size can be decreased over the limitation by SetPositionSize() or SetSize().

briefdescription.para: Sets the maximized state of a given window with specific size.

detaileddescription.para: If this function is called with true, window will be resized with screen size. Otherwise window will be resized with restore size. It is for the window's MAX button in window's border.
It is for client application. If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

parameterdescription.para: If window is maximized or unmaximized.
This is the size used when unmaximized.

briefdescription.para: Query whether window is rotating or not.

simplesect.para: true if window is rotating, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the last key event the window gets.

simplesect.para: The last key event the window gets.

briefdescription.para: Gets the last touch event the window gets.

simplesect.para: The last touch event the window gets.
It returns the raw event the window gets. There is no hit-actor and local position information.

briefdescription.para: Gets the last hover event the window gets.

simplesect.para: The last hover event the window gets.
It returns the raw event the window gets. There is no hit-actor and local position information.

briefdescription.para: Gets the last pan gesture state the window gets.

simplesect.para: The last pan gesture state the window gets.

briefdescription.para: Sets the pointer constraints lock.

simplesect.para: Returns true if PointerConstraintsLock succeeds.

briefdescription.para: Sets the pointer constraints unlock.

simplesect.para: Returns true if PointerConstraintsUnlock succeeds.

memberdef.argsstring: (Window window, int32_t x, int32_t y, int32_t width, int32_t height)
memberdef.name: LockedPointerRegionSet

briefdescription.para: Sets the locked pointer region.
Sets the locked pointer cursor position hintset.
Sets the pointer warp. The pointer moves to the set coordinates.

simplesect.para: Returns true if PointerWarp succeeds.

briefdescription.para: Sets visibility on/off of cursor.
Requests grab key events according to the requested device subtype.

simplesect.para: Returns true if KeyboardGrab succeeds.

briefdescription.para: Requests ungrab key events.

simplesect.para: Returns true if KeyboardUnGrab succeeds.

briefdescription.para: Sets full screen sized window. If full screen size is set for the window, window will be resized with full screen size. In addition, the full screen sized window's z-order is the highest.

parameterdescription.para: true to set fullscreen, false to unset.

briefdescription.para: Gets whether the full screen sized window or not.

simplesect.para: Returns true if the full screen sized window is.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables front buffer rendering.

parameterdescription.para: true to enable front buffer rendering, false to disable.

briefdescription.para: Gets whether front buffer rendering is enabled.

simplesect.para: Whether front buffer rendering has been enabled or not.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables the modal of window.

parameterdescription.para: true to enable the modal of window, false to disable.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether the window has a modal or not.

simplesect.para: True if the window is modal, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables the window always is on top.

detaileddescription.para: This is valid between windows that have no notification level or a notification level of 'none'. If it has a notification level, this will not do anything.

parameterdescription.para: true to enable the window always is on top, false to disable.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether the window always is on top.

simplesect.para: True if the window always is on top, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables the window's layer is changed to bottom.

detaileddescription.para: If the enable flag is true, this window will be placed below other windows. Otherwise, if it's called with a false value, it will be located above other windows.

parameterdescription.para: true to change the window layer to the bottom.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether the window layer is the bottom or not.

simplesect.para: True if the window layer is the bottom, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the native buffer of the window.

detaileddescription.para:
When users call this function, it wraps the actual type used by the underlying window system.
The native buffer of the Window or an empty handle

briefdescription.para: Requests relative motion grab.

parameterdescription.para: The Pointer edge boundary for grab.

simplesect.para: True if the request was successful, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Requests relative motion ungrab.

simplesect.para: True if the request was successful, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets the window blur.
Gets the window blur.

simplesect.para: Current window blur information.

briefdescription.para: Gets the window insets for all parts of the system UI.

simplesect.para: The window insets from all parts.

briefdescription.para: Gets the combined window insets for the specified parts of the system UI.

parameterdescription.para: A bitwise combination of WindowInsetsPartFlags values specifying which window insets parts to include.

simplesect.para: The combined window insets from the specified parts.

detaileddescription.para: Set the screen for this window
This method sets the window to the specified screen. The window will be moved to the specified screen.

parameterdescription.para: The window wants to change the screen.
The name of the screen for this window

detaileddescription.para: Get the screen of window. It is for multiple screen environment.

simplesect.para: The name of the screen as a string.

briefdescription.para: Request to rendering forcibly for this window.

parameterdescription.para: The number of frames to render forcibly.

briefdescription.para: The user would connect to this signal to intercept a KeyEvent at window.

detaileddescription.para: Intercepts KeyEvents in the window before dispatching KeyEvents to the control. If a KeyEvent is consumed, no KeyEvent is delivered to the control.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the mouse in or out event is received.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::MouseInOutEventevent);

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when window insets are changed by appearing or disappearing indicator, virtual keyboard, or clipboard.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the mouse relative event is received.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::MouseRelativeEventevent);

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when pointer is locked/unlocked.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the key event monitor is received.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to
